 {"id":"bd-10ufs","title":"EPIC: Fleet Management Commands (scale, rebalance, review-queue)","description":"## Goal
Provide a cohesive set of commands for managing agent fleet composition, workload distribution, and batch operations.

## Commands in Scope

### ntm scale
Manual scaling to target agent counts without autonomous auto-scaling.
- `ntm scale <session> --cc=N --cod=N --gmi=N`
- Computes delta vs current counts and calls ntm add/kill
- Safe confirmations for scale-down
- Never auto-kills user pane

### ntm rebalance
Manual rebalance suggestions when agents join/leave or workloads become uneven.
- `ntm rebalance <session>` shows imbalance and suggests reassignments
- `ntm rebalance <session> --apply` applies after confirmation
- Detects dead agents and suggests orphan task reassignment

### ntm review-queue
Suggests review prompts for idle agents to keep them productive.
- `ntm review-queue <session>` lists idle agents + recommended prompts
- `ntm review-queue <session> --send` dispatches after confirmation
- Configurable idle threshold and review templates

### ntm send --batch
Explicit batch prompt delivery from files.
- `ntm send <session> --batch file.txt` sends prompts in order
- Supports comments (#) and separators (---) in batch files
- Agent targeting: --filter, --agent, --broadcast

## Design Philosophy
- All commands are **manual** (user-initiated), not autonomous
- All modifications require **confirmation** (unless --force)
- All commands support **--dry-run** for safe preview
- All commands support **--format json** for robot mode
- Graceful degradation when assignment tracking unavailable

## Success Criteria
- Commands work without assignment tracking (basic mode)
- Commands enhance with assignment tracking when available
- Clear, deterministic output for same input state
- No background daemons or automatic triggers
- Comprehensive logging for debugging","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-11asd","title":"Task: Write unit tests for mode-to-agent assignment strategies","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Assignment Strategies

## Location
internal/ensemble/assignment_test.go

## Test Coverage

### Round-Robin Tests
```go
func TestAssignRoundRobin_EvenDistribution(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignRoundRobin_MoreModesThanPanes(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignRoundRobin_MorePanesThanModes(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignRoundRobin_SinglePane(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignRoundRobin_Determinism(t *testing.T)
```

### Category-Affinity Tests
```go
func TestAssignByCategory_PreferredTypeAvailable(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignByCategory_FallbackToAlternate(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignByCategory_NoPreferredAvailable(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignByCategory_AllCategories(t *testing.T) // Test A-L
func TestAssignByCategory_Determinism(t *testing.T)
```

### Explicit Assignment Tests
```go
func TestAssignExplicit_ValidSpecs(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignExplicit_InvalidModeID(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignExplicit_InvalidPaneType(t *testing.T)
func TestAssignExplicit_MixedIDAndCode(t *testing.T) // 'A1:cc,deductive:cod'
```

### Validation Tests
```go
func TestValidateAssignments_Valid(t *testing.T)
func TestValidateAssignments_MissingModes(t *testing.T)
func TestValidateAssignments_DuplicatePanes(t *testing.T)
func TestValidateAssignments_InvalidAgentType(t *testing.T)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log mode-to-pane assignments
- Log fallback decisions when preferred type unavailable
- Log tie-breaking rationale

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All strategies tested for correctness
- [ ] Determinism verified (same inputs -> same outputs)
- [ ] Edge cases covered (empty inputs, single item, etc.)
- [ ] Error conditions tested
- [ ] Logs comprehensive for debugging

-{"id":"bd-11mtt","title":"Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset","description":"# Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset

## Purpose
Ship the **complete 80-mode taxonomy** from `modes_of_reasoning.md` in `internal/ensemble/modes.go`, with tiered visibility for UX filtering. The catalog must be fully usable without external files.

- **core tier (~28)**: curated default set for UX
- **advanced tier (remaining modes)**: hidden by default but fully accessible via `--all` / `--tier`

## Core Tier (Curated 28)

> These are `tier: core` and must have complete metadata + hand-authored preambles.
> **Mode codes and categories must match the numbering in `modes_of_reasoning.md` (A–L).**
> The groupings below are advisory; final category assignment must follow the doc.

- deductive — Deductive Inference
- mathematical-proof — Mathematical Proof
- formal-verification — Formal Verification
- type-theoretic — Type-Theoretic Reasoning
- inductive — Inductive Generalization
- conceptual-blending — Conceptual Blending
- analogical — Analogical Transfer
- ambiguity-detection — Ambiguity Detection
- edge-case — Edge Case Reasoning
- prototype-reasoning — Prototype Reasoning
- root-cause — Root Cause Analysis
- dependency-mapping — Dependency Mapping
- argument-mapping — Argument Mapping
- adversarial-review — Adversarial Review
- systems-thinking — Systems Thinking
- worst-case — Worst-Case Analysis
- decision-under-uncertainty — Decision Under Uncertainty
- failure-mode — Failure Mode Analysis
- second-order-effects — Second-Order Effects
- resource-allocation — Resource Allocation
- strategic-planning — Strategic Planning
- option-generation — Option Generation
- prioritization — Prioritization
- engineering-design — Engineering Design Reasoning
- test-plan — Test Plan Mode
- compliance — Compliance Lens
- meta-cognitive — Meta-cognitive Monitoring
- perspective-taking — Perspective Taking

## Advanced Tier (Remaining 52)

- All remaining modes from `modes_of_reasoning.md` are included as `tier: advanced`.
- Categories must remain within A–L taxonomy.
- ModeCode **letter must match category** (A→Formal, B→Ampliative, ... L→Meta).

## Implementation Pattern
```go
var EmbeddedModes = []ReasoningMode{
    {
        ID:        "deductive",
        Code:      "A1",
        Tier:      TierCore,
        Name:      "Deductive Inference",
        Category:  CategoryFormal,
        ShortDesc: "Classical logical inference",
        // ...
    },
    // ... all 80 modes
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **All 80 modes** defined with complete metadata
- [ ] Core tier is exactly the curated 28 list above
- [ ] Advanced modes included and validated (no feature loss)
- [ ] Codes unique and category letters correct
- [ ] Categories limited to A–L taxonomy
- [ ] Modes validated at init time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:09.088788198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:59:23.825515210Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:59:23.825493889Z","close_reason":"Implemented full 80-mode embedded catalog in modes.go with 28 core + 52 advanced tier modes. All modes mapped to A-L taxonomy codes. DefaultCatalog() helper and CatalogVersion constant added. 11 comprehensive tests verify: count, tier distribution, validation, uniqueness, category-letter consistency, all categories present, code-to-ID lookup, and ListDefault correctness.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:33:01.350316238Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T09:33:43.770208854Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-11y0m","title":"LimitDetector: Capture pane output via tmux capture-pane","description":"## LimitDetector: Capture Pane Output via tmux capture-pane

### Purpose
Implement the pane output capture functionality that feeds pattern matching.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Function:**
```go
// capturePaneOutput captures the last N lines from a pane.
func (d *LimitDetector) capturePaneOutput(sessionPane string, lines int) (string, error) {
    // sessionPane format: "session_name:window.pane" (e.g., "cc_agents_1:1.3")
    
    // Use /usr/bin/tmux to avoid zsh plugin issues
    args := []string{
        "capture-pane",
        "-t", sessionPane,
        "-p",                          // Print to stdout
        fmt.Sprintf("-S", "-%d", lines), // Start line (negative = from end)
    }
    
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "/usr/bin/tmux", args...)
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("capture-pane failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    return string(output), nil
}
```

**Integration with Existing Code:**
The `internal/tmux/session.go` already has capture-pane functionality. Reuse that:
```go
output, err := tmux.CapturePaneOutput(sessionPane, 50)
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log capture: `[LimitDetector] capture_pane session_pane=%s lines=%d bytes=%d`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capture last N lines from pane
- [ ] Proper session:window.pane addressing
- [ ] Use /usr/bin/tmux directly
- [ ] Handle capture failures gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:54.713444511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427395555Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:17.427347334Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.392125380Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11y0m","depends_on_id":"bd-rt9vb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.149105820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-120q6","title":"Subtask: Define Agent State Types and Interfaces","description":"## Subtask: Define Agent State Types and Interfaces

### Purpose
Create the foundational Go types that represent agent state. These types are used throughout the entire usage limit detection system.

### File to Create
`internal/agent/types.go`

### Types to Define

```go
package agent

import "time"

// AgentType identifies which CLI agent is running
type AgentType string

const (
    AgentTypeClaudeCode AgentType = "cc"   // Claude Code CLI
    AgentTypeCodex      AgentType = "cod"  // Codex CLI (OpenAI)
    AgentTypeGemini     AgentType = "gmi"  // Gemini CLI (Google)
    AgentTypeUnknown    AgentType = "unknown"
)

// String returns the agent type as string
func (t AgentType) String() string {
    return string(t)
}

// DisplayName returns human-readable name
func (t AgentType) DisplayName() string {
    switch t {
    case AgentTypeClaudeCode:
        return "Claude Code"
    case AgentTypeCodex:
        return "Codex CLI"
    case AgentTypeGemini:
        return "Gemini CLI"
    default:
        return "Unknown"
    }
}

// AgentState represents parsed state from agent terminal output
type AgentState struct {
    // Identity
    Type     AgentType `json:"agent_type"`
    ParsedAt time.Time `json:"parsed_at"`
    
    // Quantitative metrics (nil = unknown)
    ContextRemaining *float64 `json:"context_remaining,omitempty"` // 0-100%
    TokensUsed       *int64   `json:"tokens_used,omitempty"`
    MemoryMB         *float64 `json:"memory_mb,omitempty"`
    
    // Qualitative state flags
    IsWorking     bool `json:"is_working"`      // Actively producing
    IsRateLimited bool `json:"rate_limited"`    // Hit usage cap
    IsContextLow  bool `json:"context_low"`     // Below threshold
    IsIdle        bool `json:"is_idle"`         // Waiting for input
    IsInError     bool `json:"is_in_error"`     // Error detected
    
    // Evidence for debugging
    WorkIndicators  []string `json:"work_indicators,omitempty"`
    LimitIndicators []string `json:"limit_indicators,omitempty"`
    RawSample       string   `json:"raw_sample,omitempty"`
    
    // Confidence in this assessment (0.0-1.0)
    Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"`
}

// Recommendation is the action to take based on state
type Recommendation string

const (
    RecommendDoNotInterrupt     Recommendation = "DO_NOT_INTERRUPT"
    RecommendSafeToRestart      Recommendation = "SAFE_TO_RESTART"  
    RecommendContextLowContinue Recommendation = "CONTEXT_LOW_CONTINUE"
    RecommendRateLimitedWait    Recommendation = "RATE_LIMITED_WAIT"
    RecommendErrorState         Recommendation = "ERROR_STATE"
    RecommendUnknown            Recommendation = "UNKNOWN"
)

// GetRecommendation derives recommended action from state
func (s *AgentState) GetRecommendation() Recommendation {
    // Priority order matters!
    if s.IsRateLimited {
        return RecommendRateLimitedWait
    }
    if s.IsInError {
        return RecommendErrorState
    }
    if s.IsWorking {
        if s.IsContextLow {
            return RecommendContextLowContinue // Let finish, then restart
        }
        return RecommendDoNotInterrupt
    }
    if s.IsIdle {
        return RecommendSafeToRestart
    }
    return RecommendUnknown
}
```

### Design Decisions Documented

1. **Why pointer types for metrics?** - `*float64` instead of `float64` allows distinguishing "unknown" (nil) from "zero" (0.0). Codex gives explicit percentage, Claude does not.

2. **Why Recommendation is derived, not stored?** - State can be complex; deriving recommendation ensures consistency and allows priority ordering.

3. **Why include RawSample?** - Debugging. When a decision seems wrong, we can inspect what the parser saw.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All types compile
- [ ] JSON serialization works correctly
- [ ] GetRecommendation priority order is correct
- [ ] Types are documented with comments","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:57:59.929043727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T01:36:08.392095368Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T01:36:08.392013794Z","close_reason":"Implemented AgentState types, AgentType enum, Recommendation type, Parser interface, and ParserConfig in internal/agent/types.go. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-120q6","depends_on_id":"bd-2iw6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-20T21:57:59.958540225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-121pa","title":"Task: Implement EnsemblePanel component for dashboard in internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ensemble.go","description":"# Task: Implement EnsemblePanel for Dashboard

## Location
internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ensemble.go

## Purpose
Dashboard panel showing real-time ensemble status with mode assignments, tier badges, agent progress, and quick actions.

## Panel Design
```
┌─ Reasoning Ensemble ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Question: What architectural problems exist?             │
│ Preset: architecture-review    Started: 5m ago           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ◉ cc_1  🔍 B13 ABDUCTIVE   [ADV] ██████████  done   ✓    │
│ ◉ cc_2  ⚔️  F2  ADVERSARIAL[CORE]████████░░  78% working │
│ ◉ cod_1 🔧 F40 MECHANISTIC[ADV] █████░░░░░  45% working │
│ ○ cod_2 🔄 F39 COUNTERFACT[ADV] ░░░░░░░░░░  0% pending │
│ ◉ gmi_1 🌐 F43 SYSTEMS    [CORE]███████░░░  65% working │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Progress: 3/5 active  │  Convergence: analyzing...       │
│ Advanced modes active (2) — press V for details          │
│ [S]ynthesize  [V]iew outputs  [R]efresh  [?]Help        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation
```go
package panels

type EnsemblePanel struct {
    session    *ensemble.EnsembleSession
    width      int
    height     int
    focused    bool
    refreshing bool
    // Styling
    theme      *theme.Theme
    styles     EnsemblePanelStyles
}

func NewEnsemblePanel() *EnsemblePanel { ... }

func (p *EnsemblePanel) Init() tea.Cmd {
    return p.fetchEnsembleStatus
}

func (p *EnsemblePanel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (Panel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case EnsembleStatusMsg:
        p.session = msg.Session
        return p, nil
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        switch msg.String() {
        case 's', 'S':
            return p, p.triggerSynthesis
        case 'v', 'V':
            return p, p.viewOutputs
        case 'r', 'R':
            return p, p.fetchEnsembleStatus
        }
    }
    return p, nil
}
```

### renderAssignment
- Show **mode code** + **tier badge** (`CORE`/`ADV`)
- Color tier badge subtly (core=green, adv=yellow)
- If advanced modes present, show warning line in footer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows all mode assignments with status
- [ ] Progress indicators update in real-time
- [ ] Mode icons, codes, and tier badges shown
- [ ] Advanced-mode warning shown when applicable
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work (S, V, R)
- [ ] Responsive to panel size changes
- [ ] Handles missing ensemble gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:23.888127444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:56.669843473Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:53:56.669820079Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-121pa","depends_on_id":"bd-2pb6e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:53:11.584872136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Synthesis system (bd-2qwm8)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:19:46.849106257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:03:32.644041190Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n3ti","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:03:21.801470692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n3ti","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:03:32.644003529Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n3ti","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:19:46.881463528Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nde","title":"Add ntm spawn --assign convenience flag","description":"## Overview
Provide the only convenience entry for assignment: `ntm spawn --assign`. Spawns agents and immediately assigns work in one command.

## Usage
```bash
# Spawn and assign with default strategy
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign

# With specific strategy
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=2 --assign --strategy=dependency

# With init prompt first
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign --init-prompt='Read AGENTS.md first'

# With assignment limit
ntm spawn myproject --cc=4 --assign --limit=8
```

## Workflow
1. **Spawn Phase**: Create agents via normal spawn logic
2. **Ready Wait**: Wait for all agents to show ready prompt (configurable timeout)
3. **Init Phase** (optional): Send init prompt (marching orders) to all agents
4. **Assign Phase**: Invoke `ntm assign --auto` with selected strategy
5. **Report**: Return combined spawn + assign result

## Combined Output Format
```json
{
  "spawn": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "agents": [
      {"pane": 2, "type": "claude", "status": "ready"},
      {"pane": 3, "type": "claude", "status": "ready"}
    ],
    "total": 4
  },
  "init": {
    "prompt_sent": true,
    "agents_reached": 4
  },
  "assign": {
    "strategy": "balanced",
    "assigned": [...],
    "skipped": [...],
    "summary": {...}
  }
}
```

## Ready Detection
- Watch for agent CLI prompt (e.g., "claude>" or "codex>")
- Timeout after 60s (configurable via --ready-timeout)
- On timeout: Warn and continue with ready agents

## Error Handling
- Spawn failure: Return spawn error, don't attempt assign
- Init prompt failure: Log warning, continue to assign
- Assign failure: Return partial result with assign error
- Partial success: Report which agents succeeded/failed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --assign flag works on spawn
- [ ] Waits for agent ready state
- [ ] Sends init prompt if specified
- [ ] Uses ntm assign internally (no duplicate logic)
- [ ] Strategy flag plumbed through
- [ ] --limit flag plumbed through
- [ ] Combined JSON output includes all phases
- [ ] Graceful handling of partial failures
- [ ] Clear logging of each phase

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Flag parsing and validation
- Unit test: Ready detection logic
- Unit test: Error handling for each phase
- E2E test: Full spawn + assign workflow
- E2E test: Partial failure scenarios","notes":"Keep as the single convenience entry: spawn agents + assign via ntm assign strategy. Avoid adding separate swarm/distribute entry points.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:35:43.870375746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:37.388916144Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:42:37.388808391Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nde","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:19:42.783755006Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3nde","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:03:39.620103899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Mock session/pane structure

## Logging Requirements

 {"id":"bd-3no20","title":"LimitDetector: Pattern matching with configurable regex","description":"## LimitDetector: Pattern Matching with Configurable Regex

### Purpose
Implement pattern matching logic that detects usage limits in captured pane output.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Pattern Matching:**
```go
// PatternMatcher checks output against limit patterns.
type PatternMatcher struct {
    // patterns maps agent type to compiled regexes
    patterns map[string][]*regexp.Regexp
}

// NewPatternMatcher creates matcher from config.
func NewPatternMatcher(limitPatterns map[string][]string) *PatternMatcher {
    pm := &PatternMatcher{
        patterns: make(map[string][]*regexp.Regexp),
    }
    
    for agentType, patternStrs := range limitPatterns {
        for _, p := range patternStrs {
            // Use case-insensitive matching
            re := regexp.MustCompile("(?i)" + regexp.QuoteMeta(p))
            pm.patterns[agentType] = append(pm.patterns[agentType], re)
        }
    }
    
    return pm
}

// Match checks if output matches any pattern for the agent type.
func (pm *PatternMatcher) Match(agentType, output string) (matched bool, pattern string) {
    patterns := pm.patterns[agentType]
    for _, re := range patterns {
        if re.MatchString(output) {
            return true, re.String()
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

**Default Patterns:**
```go
var DefaultLimitPatterns = map[string][]string{
    "cc": {
        "You've hit your limit",
        "You have hit your limit",
        "usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "Please wait",
        "This conversation is getting long", // Context limit
    },
    "cod": {
        "You've hit your usage limit",
        "You have reached your usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "context limit reached",
    },
    "gmi": {
        "quota exceeded",
        "rate limit",
        "context window exceeded",
    },
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log pattern match: `[LimitDetector] pattern_matched agent_type=%s pattern=%q`
- Log no match: `[LimitDetector] no_match agent_type=%s` (debug level)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Case-insensitive pattern matching
- [ ] Configurable patterns via config file
- [ ] Return matched pattern for logging
- [ ] Support both string literal and regex patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:59.034358872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641621235Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641559559Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-11y0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.173804463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.404272120Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nson","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign command strategy selection and bead-agent matching","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the core ntm assign algorithm for strategy selection and bead-to-agent matching.

## Test Cases

### Strategy Selection
- Select balanced strategy by default
- Select speed strategy when --strategy=speed
- Select quality strategy when --strategy=quality
- Select dependency strategy when --strategy=dependency
- Select round-robin strategy when --strategy=round-robin
- Error on invalid strategy name

### Bead-to-Agent Matching
- Match beads to idle agents only
- Respect agent type filters (--cc-only, --cod-only, --gmi-only)
- Apply capability matrix for quality strategy
- Prioritize high unblock_count for dependency strategy
- Even distribution for round-robin strategy

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads available
- Zero idle agents
- More beads than agents
- More agents than beads
- Single agent, multiple beads
- Multiple agents, single bead

### Output Format
- JSON output includes assigned array
- JSON output includes skipped array with reasons
- Each assignment has pane, bead_id, agent_type

## Logging Requirements
- Log strategy selected
- Log agent pool before matching
- Log each matching decision with score/reason
- Log final assignment map

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-3o9b0","title":"Implement bead assignment integration","description":"Implement bead assignment integration with --bead flag.

## Feature
When --bead=<ID> is provided, automatically send bead assignment prompt after restart.

## Prompt Template
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: <BEAD_ID> - <DESC>.
Use br show <BEAD_ID> for details. Use ultrathink.

## Implementation
1. Fetch bead info via br show --json
2. Format prompt with bead ID and description
3. Send prompt to pane after agent is ready
4. Return bead_assigned and prompt_sent in response

## Alternative: --prompt flag
Allow custom prompt instead of bead template for flexibility.

## Error Handling
- If bead ID doesn't exist: return error before restart
- If prompt send fails: note in response but still report restart success","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:25.108162363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:35:55.692234926Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o9b0","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:25.137354277Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
